synthesis/convergence-independence.title: Pick the second dataset. Candidates: Reproducibility Project Psychology (100 studies), CodaLab/OpenML benchmark replications, cancer biology replication studies, drug-target validation pipeline. Decision criteria: extractable methodological metadata, available replication/decay outcome, sample size ≥50.
synthesis/convergence-independence.section: Test family A — replicate human result on a second dataset

synthesis/convergence-independence.title: Preregister the replication test before extraction. Same shape as Test 2 v2: ρ ≤ -0.2 same direction, p < 0.05 with permutation null, robust to confounds.
synthesis/convergence-independence.section: Test family A — replicate human result on a second dataset

synthesis/convergence-independence.title: Run the replication. 2-4 weeks per dataset.
synthesis/convergence-independence.section: Test family A — replicate human result on a second dataset

synthesis/convergence-independence.title: If replication holds, run a third dataset from a different domain. If it fails, document the McLean-Pontiff result as finance-specific.
synthesis/convergence-independence.section: Test family A — replicate human result on a second dataset

synthesis/convergence-independence.title: Pick the AI-population setup. Candidates: multi-model protein prediction (AlphaFold/ESMFold/RoseTTAFold/OmegaFold against crystal-structure ground truth), multi-LLM extraction (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus/Llama/GPT against labeled extraction), multi-classifier ensemble on standard image benchmarks.
synthesis/convergence-independence.section: Test family B — direct test on AI-model populations

synthesis/convergence-independence.title: Preregister: substrate-overlap should correlate *negatively* with agreement-with-ground-truth (high-overlap pairs agree more with each other but less with truth).
synthesis/convergence-independence.section: Test family B — direct test on AI-model populations

synthesis/convergence-independence.title: Assemble the corpus. Most of the work; 4-8 weeks.
synthesis/convergence-independence.section: Test family B — direct test on AI-model populations

synthesis/convergence-independence.title: Run the analysis. Pass criterion: AI-population slope opposite-sign to Test 2 v2 human-population slope.
synthesis/convergence-independence.section: Test family B — direct test on AI-model populations

synthesis/convergence-independence.title: Defer until A and B confirm the basic effect. Synthetic-population work is expensive and only valuable once the human/AI asymmetry is established.
synthesis/convergence-independence.section: Test family C — synthetic populations to isolate mechanism

synthesis/convergence-independence.title: Design parametric synthetic populations: vary training-data overlap, objective-function diversity, error-decorrelation, post-hoc filtering, independently. Identify which variables produce the sign flip.
synthesis/convergence-independence.section: Test family C — synthetic populations to isolate mechanism

synthesis/convergence-independence.title: Conditional on test family C identifying the mechanism. Build prototypes per dominant candidate: disjoint-data ensembles, heterogeneous-objective ensembles, error-decorrelated ensembles, post-hoc filtering systems.
synthesis/convergence-independence.section: Engineering — replicate human-style convergence in AI

synthesis/convergence-independence.title: Long-horizon. Multiple years of work. Sits as Phase 3 strategic option, not Phase 2 commitment.
synthesis/convergence-independence.section: Engineering — replicate human-style convergence in AI

synthesis/convergence-independence.title: After test family B first result: same.

synthesis/lensing.title: merge with NSM somehow, we should include nsm into this, a general lensing for humans? Could be other then; aliens, dogs, patents.

synthesis/mechanics as instrument.title: **Pick canonical terminology.** Corpus uses *instrument*, *measurer*, *transducer*, *prediction pipeline*, *computational measurer*, *measurement source* roughly interchangeably. Recommendation: *instrument* for the architectural category, *measurer* for the role-in-coupling-entry (`measurer_id` field), *transducer* for analog-to-digital specifically (LLM, embedding), *prediction pipeline* for computational measurers when source tag is `predict()`. Audit and apply consistently across flagship, satellites, code, patent.

**Add a named statement of the principle to the flagship paper.** Currently scattered across `findings-for-gemini.md` Part 9, flagship §9 conclusion, Satellite 2 §2.1. Recommendation: §2 or §9, ~150 words plus catalog list. Citeable from satellites and from the patent specification.
**Surface the catalog as a reference table.** Either in the flagship or in `canvas-architecture-design.md` §1. Each row: kind, example, source-tag default, depends-chain pattern, paper reference.

**Audit `depends` chains across instrument kinds.** Physical instruments and computational pipelines have well-formed chains. Text/LLM transducers, embedding models, survey instruments, and the framework's own analysers may have inconsistent or thin chains. Document gaps and pick a canonical pattern per kind.

**Verify every claimed instrument actually produces a number.** Patent-as-instrument and code-as-instrument are mentioned in synthesis material (`patent-comparison-test-setup.md`) but the numerical-output discipline is less mature than for physical / computational kinds. If patent extraction produces only categorical labels, the framework needs to either (a) define the numerical projection explicitly, or (b) drop patent/code from the catalog.

**Verify framework analysers as instruments.** When `analyze_measurer_correlation()` emits a systemic-crack pattern, is that a coupling entry on the analyser as measurer, or registry metadata? If the former, document the schema; if the latter, decide whether to promote to first-class.

**Layer 3 synthesis is the LLM-specific child.** Cross-reference both directions: Mechanics-as-Instrument names the general principle and points at Layer 3 for LLM details; Layer 3 references back as the parent claim.

**AlphaFold Cancel Edge synthesis is superseded** by attribute-set arithmetic per Layer 3. Confirm the cross-reference chain is clean: Mechanics-as-Instrument → Layer 3 → Satellite 4 (AlphaFold). The Cancel Edge synthesis Index already says "superseded"; verify no other doc still cites it as live.

**Decide where instrument-as-target documentation lives.** Currently in Layer 3 synthesis as part of the LLM treatment, but the dual representation is general (AF/ESMFold validated it on protein predictors first). Consider promoting the dual-representation passage to Mechanics-as-Instrument and trimming Layer 3 to LLM-specific extension.

**Framing B is exactly this principle.** Confirm the patent specification (`patent-specification-v2.md`) and claim ladder (`patent-claim-ladder-v6.1.md`) state the principle in claim 1 with sufficient breadth to cover all the catalog kinds, not just physical-sensor + computational-model.

**Consider catalog extension claims** as dependent claims on Framing B. Each catalog kind (text, image, embedding, survey, patent, framework-self) is a domain embodiment that could be a dependent claim if commercial coverage requires it. Default position from `patent-claim-ladder-v2.md` is no application-domain claims; revisit if a specific kind becomes commercially central.
**Verify Framing B's three distinguishing features map cleanly onto Mechanics-as-Instrument.** Schema-level equivalence (✓
synthesis/mechanics as instrument.description: coupling-map schema is uniform across kinds), closed-loop self-conditioning (✓ — Common Ground / CFG anchoring), same-analyser-classifies-extractor symmetry (✓ — analyser as instrument). All three are corollaries of the principle, not additions to it.

synthesis/mechanics as instrument.title: **Does the principle hold for Level 5 social entities?** Surveys and LLM transducers are catalog members. Their `depends` chains are thinner than physical instruments. The Event Horizon (canvas §8.1) traps Level 5 entities inside the Epistemic canvas until physical couplings arrive. The principle still applies
synthesis/mechanics as instrument.description: surveys and LLMs are instruments — but the catalog should note the substrate-independence weakness for this kind explicitly.

synthesis/mechanics as instrument.title: **Does the framework itself becoming an instrument introduce circularity?** The framework's analysers can classify other measurers; if the analysers are themselves measurers, can they classify themselves? The trust-weight loop is the answer in principle but has not been stress-tested with self-referential cases. Document or test.

**What is the minimum `depends` chain length?** A measurer with empty `depends` is anchored to the domain's Imputed Dark Matter node (per canvas-architecture). Should the catalog require declared mechanisms, or accept undeclared with degraded triangulation strength? Current answer: accept undeclared; declare-to-earn-independence. Confirm and document.
**Update `findings-for-gemini.md` Part 9** to cross-reference this synthesis as the canonical statement.
**Update `canvas-architecture-design.md` §1** to clarify that the canvas geometry assumes the instrument principle and points at this synthesis for the definition.
**Update `paper-separation-notes.md`** if a denoising-paper-or-not decision affects which catalog kinds the standalone paper references.
**Add cross-reference in Satellite 2 §2.1** ("the human is the instrument; the LLM is the transducer") to this synthesis as the parent principle.

synthesis/ontological denoising.title: **Cross-provider convergence.** Run the same pipeline on GPT-4o, Gemini, Claude. Target κ ≥ 0.70 for "instrument-independent" claim. Difference between "Haiku does this" and "LLMs do this." Phase C.

**Non-circular CG confidence test.** Auto-construct Common Ground without replication-rate knowledge; re-run the ρ correlation. Current 0.870 is partially circular; structural-only residual is 0.730. Need a clean non-circular baseline.

**Noise-residual analysis.** 200+ (extraction − hardware) residuals from real paired data. Are residuals Gaussian? Structured? Vary by concept type? Decides whether explicit noise-model training (Phase 5 in original action plan) is warranted or whether the implicit LLM noise model is sufficient. Phase E4.
**σ ↔ replication on a wider concept set.** Current ρ = 0.619 (psych) and ρ = 0.90 (mol bio) come from small samples. Scale to the full E3 (30 concepts) or larger.

**Gärdenfors dimensional analysis.** Conceptual Spaces theory was tagged in the brain dump as a connection to make. Are the perturbation-σ extractions navigating Gärdenfors-style convex regions? Worth a short experiment.

**Scone cancel-edge dynamics over time.** Marker-passing primitives; how do they evolve as Common Ground accumulates? Connects to the AlphaFold cancel-edge synthesis (now withdrawn in favour of attribute-set arithmetic).

**Clarify what "ontological noise" formally is.** Brain dump treats it as a learnable distribution; Satellite 2 treats it operationally as σ across perturbations. The two framings should be reconciled or one chosen.

**Photograph test scaling.** Current cross-disciplinary comparison (95% camouflage rate in behavioural psychology vs 44% in physics) is single-corpus. Scale to multiple corpora per discipline; add a ground-truth annotated set for precision/recall on the camouflage classifier itself.

**Decide whether `resolution_depth` and `:sigma` distribution-shape entries should be promoted to first-class properties** with their own registry entries, vs continuing to live as schema-level metadata. Current implementation in `plyknot/guided_extraction.py::denoise_to_data_entries` and `plyknot/describe.py::distribution_to_data_entries`.

**Verify the denoising loop respects the bearing principles.** Resolution descent is a control-flow choice on the LLM measurer, not a registry→core path. The σ-as-ontological-load interpretation lives in metadata; it does not enter computation. Document this explicitly so future code review is fast.

**Naming.** Brain dump uses "Ontological Denoising"; Satellite 2 uses "classifier-free guidance for extraction"; flagship §10 uses "describe pipeline." Pick one canonical name for the framework and apply consistently. Recommendation: keep "describe pipeline" for the Plyknot-internal name, "ontological denoising" for the standalone paper, "CFG-anchored extraction" for the patent / NLP-venue framing.

**Decide whether the denoising principle gets a flagship-level paragraph** (paralleling holographic coupling and structural absence) or stays satellite-only. Argument for flagship: it is the dual to holographic coupling on the measurement side. Argument against: it is one specific extraction mechanism, not an architectural commitment.

synthesis/open-values.title: Update `notes/synthesis/lensing/lensing.md` to cross-reference open values as the parent principle (lens is one instance
synthesis/open-values.description: strain is a derived signal computed without resolving ground truth). The lensing synthesis already says "the delta is the measurement, not a side effect"; making the connection to the broader pattern explicit.

synthesis/open-values.title: Update `notes/synthesis/Holographic Coupling/holographic-coupling-synthesis.md` to cross-reference open values as the operational dual (holographic = ontological; open values = how the framework runs on the principle).

Update `notes/synthesis/Triangulation/triangulation-synthesis.md` to note that triangulation's output (graduation tier, blind-spot priority, multi-beam coverage) is consumed downstream as a derived signal under the open-values pattern.

Update `notes/synthesis/Mechanics as Instrument/mechanics-as-instrument-synthesis.md` to note that instrument outputs are consumed by analytical primitives as derived signals, not as resolved point values.
Add a brief paragraph in flagship §2 alongside structural absence: "Structural absence is the limit case of a more general principle
synthesis/open-values.description: the framework operates on derived signals (convergence status, mass, strain, marker activation, propagated σ) rather than on resolved point values. Structural absence is open values taken to non-existence." ~100 words. Documentation only.

synthesis/open-values.title: Verify the principle is reflected in the convergence-status passage of §2 ("each claim has a convergence status: ● Converged / ◐ Tension / ○ Divergent / ◌ Single"). The status is exactly the kind of derived signal the principle names; making the connection explicit would strengthen the architecture exposition.

**σ-propagation correctness audit.** Already on the Plyknot tracker per `notes/synthesis/act-verb/phase-1.5-claude-code-instructions.md` Phase 1.5 Test 1. Open values relies on σ propagation being algebraically correct; if any propagation site treats correlated inputs as independent, one of the five mechanisms collapses. Confirm the Phase 1.5 audit is scheduled and that fixing it is on the path to v1.0.

**Marker passing on cyclic depends graphs.** Phase 1.5 Test 3. If the marker passer doesn't terminate on cycles, the open-values pattern fails for any case with a cyclic dependency. Confirm scheduling.

**Verify all analytical primitives consume derived signals only.** Quick audit: does any analyser read raw coupling values as input rather than the convergence status / mass / pattern? If yes, that analyser is a candidate for refactoring under the open-values discipline. Default expectation: all analysers already conform; the audit is a safety check.

**Are there cases where ground-truth resolution is required?** The framework's claim is that analytical primitives operate on derived signals. Are there exceptions? Echo-chamber classifier needs at least one experimental measurement (a measure-tagged entry) to test prediction-only consensus, but it doesn't need that measurement to be ground truth
synthesis/open-values.description: it just needs it to be on the measurement side of the source-tag firewall. Document or test.

synthesis/open-values.title: **What is the relationship between open values and the act() verb's reflexive output?** Act produces source=action entries that are themselves coupling-map entries; the open-values principle should apply uniformly across all four verbs (measure / predict / act / describe). Confirm that act's outputs are consumed as derived signals downstream, not as resolved facts about the world.

**Naming check.** "Open values" is descriptive but unusual. Alternatives considered: "computation without resolution," "derived-signal computation," "functional-form values." The name should be evaluated by Klas; if a different framing reads better, swap before the synthesis lands in flagship §2 cross-references.

synthesis/patent-fto.title: ExtractionSchema for patent domain
synthesis/patent-fto.description: override default cognitive/sensory categories with patent-domain verb set: `['comprises', 'produces', 'couples', 'distinguishes', 'depends_on', 'enables', 'prevents', 'transforms']`. Pass to `extract_unconditioned` and `extract_conditioned` in `measure_lensing_strain`.

synthesis/patent-fto.title: Real API integration
synthesis/patent-fto.description: replace `extract_unconditioned_simulated` / `extract_conditioned_simulated` with the production functions. Supply patent-domain `ExtractionSchema`. Cost < $0.10 per pair on Haiku.

synthesis/patent-fto.title: IDS sweep
synthesis/patent-fto.description: extend `evaluate_pair` loop over all ~20 IDS references, not just Palantir US 11,714,792. Write the result aggregator (per-reference verdict table, crack/opening/tension summary).

synthesis/patent-fto.title: Multi-extractor stabilisation
synthesis/patent-fto.description: run N extractors per document, reconcile into a `StabilisedExtraction` via N-ordering. `common_ground.stabilise` is not in the framework; write a local reconciliation helper.

synthesis/patent-fto.title: Extraction stability gate
synthesis/patent-fto.description: wire `DOCUMENT_EXTRACTION_STABILITY` into `evaluate_pair`. Multi-run extraction required to compute the property. Threshold < 0.85 → escalate.

synthesis/patent-fto.title: `substrate_overlap_matrix`
synthesis/patent-fto.description: restore `StabilisedExtraction.substrate_overlap_matrix` population. `measurers.substrate_overlap` function exists but is not called in the wired pipeline.

synthesis/patent-fto.title: **Stage 3 verification 5.1**
synthesis/patent-fto.description: Claims 18–19 parse check (5 min). Confirm why those claims extract zero predicates; adjust parser if needed. Blocker for any per-claim mass interpretation on claims 18–19.

synthesis/patent-fto.title: **Stage 3 verification 5.2**
synthesis/patent-fto.description: Bekenstein code spot-check (10 min). Open the relevant code module(s) and confirm whether the framework actually computes something Bekenstein-related or whether extraction caught a comment/docstring artifact. If real, flag in IP strategy notes as continuation candidate; if artifact, dismiss. Blocker for treating the Strategic Shear list as actionable.

synthesis/patent-fto.title: **Stage 3 verification 5.3**
synthesis/patent-fto.description: Predicate fidelity spot-check on 4 documents (30–45 min). Manual review of extracted predicates vs. source text for a representative sample. Blocker for treating any Stage 3 numerics as authoritative. Run before 5.4 and 5.5.

synthesis/patent-fto.title: **Stage 3 verification 5.4**
synthesis/patent-fto.description: Manual Palantir walkthrough (45–60 min). Console-investigation of claim_01 × ref_v6_2_1 (Lensing Strain 0.91, highest-strain pair). Blocker for synthesis. Depends on 5.3.

synthesis/patent-fto.title: **Stage 3 verification 5.5**
synthesis/patent-fto.description: Two patent cracks manual walkthrough (30–45 min). Pick two (claim, reference, shared-dep) tuples from `outputs/stage3-patent-cracks.json` and assess: real legal exposure or marker-passing artifact from thin predicate mapping? Blocker for sending crack list to patent attorney. Depends on 5.3.

synthesis/patent-fto.title: **Stage 3.1**
synthesis/patent-fto.description: Add paper + validation beams to phenomenological mass scores. Promotes some YELLOW claims to GREEN; sharpens "which claims are most defensible" picture. Run after verifications 5.1–5.5 resolve favorably.

synthesis/patent-fto.title: **Patent-FTO synthesis document**
synthesis/patent-fto.description: Three sections: (1) what Stages 1–3 demonstrated, (2) holographic-coupling architectural framing, (3) remaining Stage 3.1 / Stage 3.5 work. Depends on verifications; not yet.

synthesis/patent-fto.title: **Methodological refinement**
synthesis/patent-fto.description: Two-report-per-phase discipline (API discovery report + results report, separate files). Carry into future stages; also relevant to Satellite 2.

synthesis/patent-fto.title: Stage 1 wiring
synthesis/patent-fto.description: all stubs replaced with framework primitives, adapter package created, pipeline rewritten, smoke test passes (2026-04-25)

synthesis/product concept-distance.title: Build Epistemic Canvas extraction pipeline for concept-level claim decomposition from uploaded texts
Implement scalar distance computation per concept (normalized 0-1 scale)
Implement dimensional decomposition showing which semantic dimensions contribute to distance
Build contributing-claim extraction with sentence-level citations from source texts
Build cross-text usage map: shared claims, Text A only, Text B only, direct contradictions
Build interactive web view for concept-distance reports
Target 30-second to 2-minute response time for most text pairs
Support up to 50,000 words per text input
Deploy as self-serve commercial product on plyknot.com
Validate on example use cases: comparative literature, translation quality, policy analysis, historical drift

synthesis/triangulation.title: Add brief mention in `canvas-architecture-design.md` §7.1 (operational triangulation) connecting to blind-spot triangulation as the discovery-side application of the same test. ~50 words.

Add brief mention in flagship §9 ("Blind spot triangulation" passage) connecting back to operational triangulation as the graduation-side application. Currently the flagship discusses blind-spot in isolation. ~50 words.
Add brief mention in `notes/synthesis/Patent-fto/patent-comparison-test-setup.md` connecting Strategic Shear / multi-beam comparison to operational triangulation as the third application. ~50 words.

Update `notes/synthesis/Mechanics as Instrument/mechanics-as-instrument-synthesis.md` to cross-reference triangulation as the primitive that operationalizes substrate-independence on the instrument side.

Update `notes/synthesis/Holographic Coupling/holographic-coupling-synthesis.md` "Where the framework already does this" section to note that triangulation is the test whose output is mass / coordinates / graduation. The synthesis already lists operational triangulation as one of the five primitives; the cross-reference to this synthesis makes the test/output relationship explicit.

**Decide whether multi-beam triangulation deserves a named function.** Stage 3 composed it from primitives with two workspace adapters (`make_patent_step` bypasses the `depends` validator for patent predicates, `predicate_to_int` bridges string→integer keys for marker passing). If patent-FTO becomes a recurring commercial use case, promoting the composition to a named analyser (`analyze_multibeam_coverage()` or similar) may be worth it. Default: leave as workspace pattern until a second use case emerges.

**Decide whether the substrate-independence test itself should be exposed as a public function.** Currently it is implicit in `analyze_measurer_correlation`, `analyze_prediction_consensus`, `find_blind_spots`, and the canvas graduation logic. A standalone `compute_substrate_independence(measurer_a, measurer_b) -> float` would centralize the logic but might be over-engineering. Default: leave distributed across consumers.

**Calibrate the priority formula in `find_blind_spots()`.** Current formula: `len(shared_deps) × (10 if existing_couplings == 0 else 1)`. The 10× multiplier is a heuristic; an empirical calibration against a held-out gap set (where the "right" answer is known retrospectively from later literature) would settle whether the multiplier is right or whether shared-dep count alone is sufficient.

**Test whether multi-beam triangulation generalizes beyond patent-FTO.** Stage 3 validated it for the patent case. The composition pattern (multiple beams against same target, cross-beam Shear analysis) should apply to any case where the target is illuminated by independent measurement processes. Candidate domains: clinical-trial cross-modality (biomarker beam + outcome beam + radiology beam), pharmaceutical lead optimization (in-vitro beam + in-silico beam + cellular beam), AI evaluation (benchmark beam + red-team beam + deployment beam). Worth one validation run on a non-patent target before claiming generality.
**Confirm "triangulation" is the right name.** The geometric metaphor is loose
synthesis/triangulation.description: two-measurer cases are common, multi-beam cases can be arbitrarily many. Alternative: "substrate-independence test" (precise but jargon-heavy), "depends-overlap analysis" (precise but flat). Default: keep "triangulation" since it is already used in both canvas §7.1 and flagship §9; the metaphor is established.

synthesis/triangulation.title: **Document that triangulation is a primitive, not a top-level concept.** The synthesis says this in its frame; the Index says it. Worth a one-sentence reminder in the canvas-architecture-design overview that distinguishes top-level concepts (mass, lensing, fission, event horizon) from primitives (triangulation, marker passing, convergence).
